Zoning By-Law Amendment and Site Plan Control application to permit a mixed-use building with two towers, 32 and 46 storeys (113.3 metres and 155.2 metres) in height, connected by an 11-storey podium. The existing buildings on the site would be demolished. The proposed development includes 770 rental units (of which 231 will be affordable), a child care facility (725 square metres), a fitness club (2,064 square metres), and ground level retail (437 square metres). A total of 200 parking spaces and 790 bicycle parking spaces are proposed to support the new development.

Site Plan Amendment to the previously approved 9-storey mixed-use building under File No. 20 165519 NNY 08 SA. The proposed revisions include a reduction in the number of residential units from 127 to 126, a decrease in the required parking spaces, and a reduction in the permitted gross floor area. In addition, the amendment seeks to increase the required indoor and outdoor amenity space while reducing the green roof area and overall coverage. These changes are intended to refine the development while maintaining the overall intent and character of the original approval.

The City of Toronto adopted Official Plan Amendment 716 (Downsview Secondary Plan) on June 27, 2024. A statutory public meeting was held at the Planning and Housing Committee on May 9, 2024. The Downsview Secondary Plan is in force and effect as of August 9, 2024, except for those lands south of Wilson Avenue which are subject to appeals to the Ontario Land Tribunal (OLT-24-000829).Please see item 2024.PH12.1 at https://secure.toronto.ca/council/agenda-item.do?item=2024.PH12.1 and the Update Downsview study website at www.toronto.ca/updatedownsview.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes the development of two 53-storey mixed-use buildings providing residential, and commercial use at grade. The buildings will provide a total of 1,424 residential units, being a mix of 1-bedroom, 1-bedroom + den, 2-bedroom, 2-bedroom + den and 3-bedroom units. A paved access from Yeomans road along the southern property line will be provided, leading to 2 levels of below-grade parking, providing a total of 489 parking spaces; as well as 642 bicycle parking spaces. Zoning By-Law Amendment and Site Plan Control application to permit a mixed-use building with two towers, 32 and 46 storeys (113.3 metres and 155.2 metres) in height, connected by an 11-storey podium. The existing buildings on the site would be demolished. The proposed development includes 770 rental units (of which 231 will be affordable), a child care facility (725 square metres), a fitness club (2,064 square metres), and ground level retail (437 square metres). A total of 200 parking spaces and 790 bicycle parking spaces are proposed to support the new development. The Zoning By-law Amendment application (19 127586 STE 13 OZ) has been approved by Council (TE24.10).

District plan, zoning by-law amendment and draft plan of subdivision applications for Taxiway District, which comprises 40.7 hectares (100.7 acres) of land consisting of hangars, offices and ancillary structures, surface parking lots, and a portion of the former Downsview Airport taxiway. Collectively, the district is proposed to accommodate approximately 489,009 square metres of GFA (282,750 square metres non-residential GFA and 206,259 square metres residential GFA) with a resulting gross density of 1.2 FSI. The proposed development will provide approximately 7,400 jobs and 2,957 dwelling units. The proposed development will maintain a predominantly mid-scale built form character with an option for two tall buildings of 19 and 23 storeys. Adaptive reuse of buildings from the previous aerospace manufacturing operations will accommodate a range of new institutional, retail, entertainment, and community uses. A range of housing options are proposed including affordable housing. 930 square metres of GFA are proposed for community uses along with a 1,547 square metre child care facility (1,001m indoor; 546 outdoor). The district plan proposes 14,295 square metres of public parkland across 3 parks, 13,844 square metres of privately-owned publicly accessible spaces, and 9,103 square metres of greenways.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes the development of a 9-storey Long Term Care Facility. The building will provide a total of 512 LTC Resident Rooms, being a mix of private and semi-private. A paved access from Guildwood Parkway will be provided, leading to pick-up/drop-off area and surface parking, providing a total of 145 parking spaces; as well as 88 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 28,612 square metres and a FSI of 0.94. Additionally, the proposed development provides indoor and outdoor amenity space.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes the development of an 46-storey mixed-use building providing residential and commercial use. The building will provide a total of 636 residential units, being a mix of studio, 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, and 3-bedroom units. The building will also provide retail at grade. A paved access from Eldora Ave along the north property line will be provided, leading to 3 levels of below-grade parking, providing a total of 154 parking spaces; as well as 370 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 39,191.50 square metres and a FSI of 15.27. Additionally, the proposed development provides 1,443.50 square metres of indoor amenity space and 469.70 square metres of outdoor amenity space.

Lift the Hold application related to a new 10-storey building, containing 80 emergency shelter beds, 100 transitional shelter beds, 124 long-term care beds, 70 supportive housing units, and a 2,269 square metre community hub. The proposal would replace the existing Seaton House men's shelter. For more information about the proposal, go to: www.toronto.ca/george-street-revitalization

The applicant proposes a logistics hub / warehouse.  The site area is 124,659.4 square metres.  The proposal is a phased development which would include one industrial warehouse with accessory office uses; total GFA is 41,824.60 square metres and height is 15.88 metres, at grade vehicular parking lot total of 639 spaces, 16 bicycle parking spaces, and 58 trailer parking spaces are proposed.   The subject lands were part of a consent application (File No.: B0009/22SC) that was granted.
